facts.fact: Eversong Woods in this era reads as a curated golden homeland rather than a raw wilderness, with Farstrider patrol roads cutting through managed forest.
The Thalassian Pass is the southern bottleneck into Quel'Thalas, where the road hardens, the air cools, and ward-stones grow denser.
The border post at the pass is built directly into the mountain flank, making stone itself part of the security environment.
Silvermoon lies north of the southeastern patrol ground and functions as the administrative pull opposite the southbound border road.
South of Thalassian Pass, elven stone and warded order give way quickly to rutted Lordaeron earth, thinning pines, ditches, standing water, and scattered farmsteads.
Millhaven sits less than an hour beyond the first abandoned farms and clusters around a narrow lane and stream where local magical currents pinch together.
North of Millhaven, the threatened road runs through Alder Run, Three Willows, and an old relay post before the border lane turns east.
Millhaven's internal geography matters tactically: a bridge, a stream, a mill, a detached granary, rear churn behind the houses, and a clearer north road beyond Paret's shed.
Two days south of Millhaven, the next investigated village is still alive and functioning, proving the road can carry early-stage sickness long before a place looks lost from outside.
The road beyond that village bends southwest toward Andorhal, and wagon ruts there read as recent heavy grain traffic rather than isolated local hauling.
Andorhal now enters the map not as distant lore but as the next visible node on the route, imagined at chapter end as a warehouse-scale grain hub.

By chapter 5 the relevant landscape is no longer a single contaminated village but the trade-road web itself: weigh houses, feeder lanes, transfer depots, and inns where wagon crews leave records behind.
The south feeder road running toward Strahnbrad becomes the chosen approach to Andorhal because the east line has gone unnaturally quiet while the south line still carries live traffic.
Strahnbrad now matters geographically as the human-war corridor their route must cross on the way into Andorhal's road network.
Chapter 6 confirms the south feeder road runs directly through Strahnbrad's military corridor before opening onto Andorhal's approach lanes.
The final rise before Andorhal gives a clear view of granary roofs, gate roads, storehouses, and exchange yards, making the city's scale legible before entry.

Chapter 7 gives Andorhal an interior geography that matters tactically: gate tables, inspection office, exchange shelves, mage-side assay rooms, repack floors, loading yards, spoilage rooms, alleys, and a granary district where covert structure hides inside ordinary flow.
Chapter 8 gives Andorhal a tactical interior: west-line streets, cooper sheds, service yards, roof crossings, and coal-bin-hidden shelters all matter as real pursuit geography.
Eastweald enters the map as the next operational theater beyond Andorhal, meaning the hunt has widened from one city into a larger stretch of Lordaeron grain country.
By chapter 8, Andorhal is geographically reclassified from suspected hub to distribution throat: the place where ordinary roads fan poisoned grain outward rather than the place where the route ends.
North of Andorhal, Eastweald farm country is being actively kept: roads clear, ditches cut back, and dead orchards trimmed for sightlines.
A single ridge in chapter 9 makes the fork explicit: Barov-held country to the east, Hearthglen to the south-west.
Chapter 10 maps the Barov line as maintained Eastweald estate country leading to Darrowmere Lake and the island fortress of Caer Darrow.
Darrowmere functions as a physical threshold: once the lake crossing begins, access narrows to the watched route into Caer Darrow.

Chapter 11 maps Eastweald as still-worked countryside whose productive surface hides organized plague occupation: blackened fields beside healthy ones, tidy villages, and shut granaries carrying treated-grain stink.
Hearthglen now sits on the active plague map as both fortified town and military staging ground, connected directly to the same grain-road system Sylvanas has been tracing.
By chapter end the Eastweald road system links three live directions at once: the south line under Arthas, the north turn back toward Darrowmere, and the still-active countryside between them.
Chapter 12 fixes the deepest physical layout: beneath Scholomance and beneath Caer Darrow sits an office-like inner sanctum where the plague's core scholarship is conducted.
The keep and academy behave as one vertical defensive space, handing motion from upper thresholds down toward the sanctum in layered ward responses.
Chapter 13 confirms Caer Darrow can be breached by the east wall rather than the proper landing, turning Darrowmere into a stealth geography instead of a simple approach route.
The hidden way down into the active core runs through service rooms and an older crypt spine rather than the polite academy route.
By chapter end the road south links Darrowmere directly to the Hearthglen and Stratholme crisis as one continuous map of urgency.

Chapter 13 fixes the covert geography of Caer Darrow: east wall quieter than the public landing, upper service window reachable from the lake, and an old crypt spine linking the upper workrooms to the sanctum below.

The south road from Darrowmere now runs under immediate relay pressure toward Hearthglen and Stratholme, making the island operation feel geographically connected to the wider war rather than isolated from it.
The road linking Darrowmere, Hearthglen, and Stratholme can still look like ordinary Eastweald farm country even after the protagonists know it is carrying city-scale plague risk.

Eastweald remains a functioning agricultural corridor deep into the crisis, with orchards, wagon traffic, hedgerow farms, and supper bells still active on the same line that now leads directly into Stratholme.
Stratholme sits on the same grain and service-road network as Hearthglen, which is why Jaina can map granaries, bakehouses, and holding yards as intervention points before arrival.
